steps.title: Use the proximity contrast
steps.content: Quito respondents reported higher park use than Lima respondents, even though fewer Quito respondents lived within a 10-minute walk of a park. The text states that greater proximity alone cannot explain the difference, so the explanation must point away from distance to parks.

steps.title: Remove unsupported explanations
steps.content: Choice A invents researcher expectations. Choice C invents survey inaccuracies. Choice D invents older sources for the calculation. The text instead says park proximity cannot account for the usage gap. The answer is B.
